label: Alert Summary
description: Active alert count, critical count, average resolution time, and 24-hour total alert count.

0.1.note: WidgetErrorBoundary wraps all widgets via widget-wrapper.tsx line 89

0.5.note: No lib/mocks imports in widget file

0.6.note: isLoading from useAlertsData() used: metric values show '—' while loading

0.7.note: Empty/zero state handled: KpiSummaryWidget renders 0 counts correctly; '0 Active Alerts' is a valid non-error state for this widget class

0.8.note: Global WidgetErrorBoundary covers runtime errors per spec 0.8

0.9.note: No dead code. AVG_RESOLUTION_DISPLAY and LAST_24H_DISPLAY are placeholder constants pending API — documented in file comment, not dead code

1.1.note: Data sourced exclusively from useAlertsData() context hook (activeCount, criticalCount, isLoading)

1.2.note: AlertsDataProvider reads useGlobalScope() and filters by scopeStrategyIds

1.3.note: allAlerts memoized on scope.organizationIds; org filter change recalculates activeCount and criticalCount

1.4.note: scope.clientIds cascades through getStrategyIdsForScope in context

1.5.note: scope.strategyIds cascades through getStrategyIdsForScope in context

1.6.note: scopeStrategyIds.length === 0 returns all alerts; widget shows full counts when no filter active

1.7.note: AVG_RESOLUTION_DISPLAY and LAST_24H_DISPLAY are module-level constants (correct placement for placeholder values awaiting API)

l1.notes: Pure display widget — reads 3 computed values from context. No direct filtering required in widget itself.

knownIssues.description: AVG_RESOLUTION_DISPLAY ('12m') and LAST_24H_DISPLAY ('23') are hardcoded placeholder constants. Real values pending alerts API response shape extension.
